name: Ascent to Lorbés — round trip from the abandoned village of Ruesta
name_original: Subida a Lorbés — ida y vuelta desde el pueblo abandonado de Ruesta
region_intro: Road cycling around Urriés offers diverse terrain within the Aragonese Cinco Villas region, characterized by its pre-Pyrenean setting. The area features rolling hills and varied topography, providing both moderate and challenging climbs suitable for road cyclists. The landscape includes river valleys, such as the Onsella River, and open countryside, contributing to a range of cycling experiences. This natural environment supports multiple possibilities for outdoor activity. Best road cycling routes around Urriés The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Yesa — circular of Sos del Rey Católico, a 50.3 miles (80.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views around the Yesa Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Sos and Castiliscar Loop, a moderate 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This loop includes climbs over a mountain pass, providing varied terrain and scenic vistas. Local road cyclists also love the Long along the Onsuella River — round trip from Sos del Rey Catolico, a 37.0 miles (59.6 km) trail leading through the Onsella River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Urriés is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and pre-Pyrenean terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Urriés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 270 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Urriés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Urriés?
A: There are 16 road cycling routes around Urriés, offering a mix of moderate and difficult terrain suitable for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Urriés?
A: The region around Urriés features diverse pre-Pyrenean terrain, including rolling hills and varied topography. You'll encounter both moderate climbs and more challenging ascents, often along river valleys like the Onsella River, providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Urriés?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling around Urriés, with milder temperatures ideal for tackling the region's varied terrain. Summers can be warm, while winters may bring colder weather.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes around Urriés?
A: The road cycling routes around Urriés are primarily classified as moderate or difficult, with no easy routes listed. Therefore, they may be more suitable for experienced cyclists or families with older, more capable riders.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling near Urriés?
A: While cycling around Urriés, you can explore several historical and cultural points of interest. These include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/934414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier&lt;/a&gt; and the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5266483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers good views of the Yesa Reservoir. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artieda&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Urriés area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992890036/embalse-de-yesa-circular-of-sos-del-rey-catolico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Yesa — circular of Sos del Rey Católico&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 80.9 km trail offering extensive views. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990790948/puerto-de-sos-and-castiliscar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sos and Castiliscar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes varied terrain and scenic vistas.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Urriés?
A: The road cycling routes in Urriés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views, and the opportunities for both moderate rides and challenging climbs within the pre-Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region's landscape includes river valleys. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992888820/long-along-the-onsuella-river-round-trip-from-sos-del-rey-catolico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long along the Onsuella River — round trip from Sos del Rey Catolico&lt;/a&gt; is a 59.6 km trail that leads through the Onsella River valley.

Q: What are the options for more challenging road cycling routes?
A: For those seeking a challenge, there are 7 difficult road cycling routes around Urriés. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992890036/embalse-de-yesa-circular-of-sos-del-rey-catolico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Yesa — circular of Sos del Rey Católico&lt;/a&gt;, at 80.9 km with over 1000m of elevation gain, is a prime example, offering extensive views and significant climbs.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Urriés?
A: Urriés is a small village, and while specific dedicated cyclist parking isn't detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the town center. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most current parking regulations.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in the Urriés area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads around Urriés. However, always respect local regulations, especially if cycling through protected natural areas or private land, and ensure you adhere to standard traffic laws.

Q: What kind of accommodation is available for cyclists in Urriés?
A: Urriés, being a charming Aragonese village, offers a selection of rural accommodations, guesthouses, and possibly small hotels. These options cater to visitors looking to experience the local culture and natural environment, making them suitable for cyclists.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants in Urriés for a post-ride meal?
A: Yes, Urriés has local amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your ride. These establishments often offer traditional Aragonese cuisine and a welcoming atmosphere.

Q: Can I reach Urriés by public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options directly to Urriés may be limited, especially for accommodating bicycles. It's recommended to check regional bus schedules or train services to nearby larger towns and then cycle the remaining distance, or consider driving to Urriés.

Q: Where can I find bike rental services near Urriés?
A: Bike rental services are not typically found directly within Urriés due to its small size. You would likely need to look for rental shops in larger towns or cities within the Aragon region, such as Zaragoza or Pamplona, and arrange transport for the bikes to Urriés.
tips: We come across a beautiful picture offered by the medieval castle of Ruesta on top of a stony hill, from where there are good views of the Yesa reservoir. It dates from the 11th century and was an important defensive enclave of the kingdom of Aragon during the Reconquest period. Currently, only two towers remain.

Located on top of a hill at an altitude of 828 metres, Lorbés is the northernmost town in the province of Zaragoza located between the Gabarre ravine and the Sacal river, next to which the ruins of the old flour mill can barely be seen. It had a population of 9 inhabitants in 2004 and 1 in 2017.
